items.claim: Persistent sessions, refresh rotation, MFA and revocation are exercised against PostgreSQL.
items.nextAction: Extend negative object-level authorization evidence without replacing the persistent authority.

items.claim: Server-side tenant and participant checks exist, but the complete 12-role negative matrix is not yet represented as one mandatory release artifact.
items.nextAction: Add a release-gated 12-role negative authorization matrix covering tenant escape, wrong object and invalid state.

items.claim: PostgreSQL-backed lot, bid, award and deal-link read authority exists; the full deterministic close and worker-restart race suite is not yet proven in the release gate.
items.nextAction: Verify bid idempotency, late-bid rejection, equal-price tie-breaking, dual winner selection and close-worker recovery on real PostgreSQL.

items.claim: Canonical Deal commands use persistent state, version CAS, idempotency, audit, outbox and ledger effects with industrial PostgreSQL tests.
items.nextAction: Preserve this authority while replacing remaining peripheral runtime reads and commands.

items.claim: The canonical Deal lifecycle persists logistics commands, but the complete conflict matrix for driver, vehicle, carrier replacement and overlapping trips is not yet a dedicated mandatory gate.
items.nextAction: Add concurrent assignment, overlapping driver/vehicle, duplicate arrival and carrier replacement integration tests.

items.claim: Laboratory lifecycle has PostgreSQL authority and industrial integration coverage.
items.nextAction: Add explicit supersede and immutable evidence-hash negative cases to the consolidated release suite.

items.claim: Durable evidence metadata exists, but WORM retention, antivirus execution and object-store restore are not proven on production-like infrastructure.
items.nextAction: Implement and test immutable retention, signed URL boundaries, malware quarantine and object metadata restore drill.

items.claim: Integer money, callback signature verification, replay safety, reconciliation and persistent settlement authority are covered by industrial tests.
items.nextAction: Keep bank integration status non-live until real partner credentials and callbacks are exercised.

items.claim: Lease, retry, dead-letter and multi-worker safety are covered in the industrial core suite.
items.nextAction: Add backlog recovery and downstream timeout evidence at production-like scale.

items.claim: Industrial observability tests exist, but the complete logs-metrics-traces-alerts contract is not yet proven against a production-like monitoring stack.
items.nextAction: Create a mandatory telemetry contract and verify correlation across API, DB, outbox, worker, callback and storage.

items.claim: CI-scale concurrency proof exists, but the required 500 RPS sustained, 1000 RPS burst, 5000 sessions, 50000 deals and 10 million events profile has not been executed.
items.nextAction: Run the full production-like load profile and retain raw results with commit, environment and artifact digest.

items.claim: CI performs database backup and restore rehearsal, but regional RPO/RTO and object-storage recovery are not measured.
items.nextAction: Measure database and object recovery on production-like infrastructure and publish machine-readable RTO/RPO evidence.

items.claim: Security workflows exist, but several scanners are informational or explicitly ignore failures, so the current gate does not enforce the stated release policy.
items.nextAction: Make Critical and unexcepted High findings blocking, remove unconditional success paths and add API fuzz, replay and tenant-isolation release gates.

items.authority: GitHub Actions plus active hosting contour
items.claim: The repository still receives failing Vercel and Deno deployment statuses on main even though those contours are prohibited or retired.
items.nextAction: Remove retired provider integrations and required checks outside the repository, then verify a clean exact-head status using the approved deployment contour.

items.claim: Bank, FGIS, EDI and government integrations must remain designed or sandbox until real credentials, partner environments and independent acceptance are available.
items.nextAction: Preserve honest capability states and execute live acceptance only after external access and agreements are supplied.
